title: Personal domain workflow families defined
objective: Define bounded, read-only-first workflow families for the ten personal domains (finance, health, legal, files, calendar, contacts, communications, knowledge, tasks, family coordination) over the governed data substrate, such that every domain begins read-only, carries explicit authority and privacy, defines DevWorld simulation and Needs-Human-Approval before any impact, and includes rollback, proof, and a single bounded next action — with no personal service or data mutated.
design_note: This file IS the design artifact named in target_artifacts (generated/task_families/PERSONAL-002.json). Each work_item is a complete per-domain workflow contract, not a summary: it carries the full set of gate clauses (begins_read_only, authority, privacy, simulation_for_impact, approval_for_impact, rollback, proof, bounded_next_action) plus the substrate families that back it. The shared invariants that every domain inherits are factored into cross_cutting_contract to avoid restating the constitution ten times.
supporting_sources: generated/task_graph.source.csv (PERSONAL-002 row: goal, verification_gate, blocked_paths, execution_cell=policy-cell, next_action)
1.0_VISION/LifeOS Master Plan — Consolidated v1 (2026-07-07).md (Constitution §6; Execution gates §11; runtime rule; cell types; simulation doctrine; MVP1 financial+legal)
generated/postgres_user_space_schema_plan.json (POSTGRES-002 substrate families the domains write onto)

cross_cutting_contract.substrate: All personal domain records live on the STORE-001 governed substrate: PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single canonical durable owner of every durable personal class; redb is the transient buffer/scratchpad/WAL/BLAKE3-dedup tier and never a second durable writer; envctl is the forward-only bridge (collector -> redb -> embed -> PostgreSQL); AgentDB (.rvf) holds non-authoritative per-agent cognition. Personal domain records map onto domain_record (domain='personal'), fs_node + blob (files/media), communication, principal + grant (identity/authority), graph_edge (relationships), and the revision spine (history/rollback), per generated/postgres_user_space_schema_plan.json.
cross_cutting_contract.authority_law: David is the final human authority; no agent outranks that layer. Per the runtime rule, no agent receives unlimited authority — every meaningful action passes through identity, policy, memory, simulation, execution, proof, and rollback. Constitution §6 binds every domain: no hidden mutation, no unauthorized spend, no legal bypass, no real-world execution without authority, no production mutation without snapshot, no completion without proof.
cross_cutting_contract.approval_gate: Execution gates §11: approval requires bounded + reversible + evidence-sufficient; automatic Needs-Human-Approval for spend, legal, external real-world action, production mutation, account creation, credential handling, trading, physically-impactful home automation, and anything irreversible. No domain may cross from read-only into impact without passing this gate.
cross_cutting_contract.cell_law: PERSONAL-002 runs in a policy-cell: network denied by default, read-only filesystem except declared writable_paths, no mutation without snapshot; promotion path is simulation -> dev-cell -> proof-cell -> release-cell. Simulation output writes constraints to the task graph, not advice, and a failed world becomes a failure capsule, never a new baseline.
cross_cutting_contract.privacy_inheritance: Each domain inherits the PERSONAL-001 personal-data privacy family: explicit ownership, consent, local-first storage, encryption, access control (grant), retention, export/sharing, deletion authority, backup, and recovery. Invariant from PERSONAL-001: no personal data leaves its boundary silently — cross-boundary movement requires an explicit consent-backed grant.
cross_cutting_contract.blocked_this_task: No health or legal action, no spend, no communication send, no credential access, no product implementation, no secrets access. This family is a planning artifact only; it accesses no personal service or data.
cross_cutting_contract.rollback_primitive: Because every domain begins read-only, there is nothing to undo at entry. Rollback is defined for the eventual approved impact: PostgreSQL PITR + the append-only revision spine (before_sha256/after_sha256, COW branch lineage) reconstruct any prior state, and 'no production mutation without snapshot' guarantees a restore point exists before any mutation. Grants/shares are revoked by writing a new revision (valid_to set), never by deletion.
cross_cutting_contract.proof_primitive: Every domain closes through a ProofRecord (no completion without proof, Constitution §6): the SimulationReport, the approval decision, the snapshot id, and the resulting revision are recorded before a workflow is considered done.
cross_cutting_contract.bounded_next_action_primitive: Per the PERSONAL-002 next_action, each domain decomposes into exactly one read-only workflow before any action — the single bounded next step, never a batch of mutations.

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (domain_record domain='personal', record_type='finance'); blob for statements/receipts; policy owner: LifeOS Product / Privacy Agent under David's authority
work_items.decision: Read-only financial workflow: ingest account balances, transactions, statements, and budgets as domain_record/blob rows; never spend, transfer, or trade without passing the §11 Needs-Human-Approval gate.
work_items.begins_read_only: Ingest-and-observe only: balances, transaction history, statements (blob content_sha256), budget lines. No spend, no transfer, no trade, no account mutation.
work_items.authority: David is final authority; Constitution §6 'no unauthorized spend'. Any spend/transfer/trade is an §11 Needs-Human-Approval action (spend, trading). Agents may propose a budget or a payment, never execute one.
work_items.privacy: Financial records are owner-scoped (owner_principal_id), encrypted at rest, local-first; per-account consent; retention policy on statements; no sharing without an explicit consent-backed grant (PERSONAL-001).
work_items.simulation_for_impact: Any proposed money movement (payment, transfer, budget change, trade) is first run as a WorldSeed in DevWorld; the SimulationReport (projected balance diffs, resource burn, failure nodes) writes constraints, not advice.
work_items.approval_for_impact: §11 gate: spend and trading are automatic Needs-Human-Approval; approval requires bounded + reversible + evidence-sufficient. No money moves without David's explicit approval on the simulated action.
work_items.rollback: Pre-mutation snapshot + PostgreSQL PITR + revision spine reconstruct prior financial state; a reversed/void transaction is a new revision, the original preserved.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord binds the SimulationReport, the approval decision, the snapshot id, and the resulting revision before any financial workflow is marked done.
work_items.bounded_next_action: Read-only ingest of account balances and transaction history into domain_record; produce a spend-free financial summary. No spend.
work_items.source_grounding: Master Plan MVP1 domain = financial; Constitution §6 (no unauthorized spend); §11 (spend, trading -> Needs-Human-Approval); POSTGRES-002 domain_record/blob; PERSONAL-001 privacy family.

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (domain_record domain='personal', record_type='health'); blob for records/imaging; policy owner: LifeOS Product / Privacy Agent under David's authority
work_items.decision: Read-only health workflow: ingest health records, metrics, medications, and appointments as domain_record/blob rows; take no health action (no booking, no provider messaging, no regimen execution) without the §11 gate.
work_items.begins_read_only: Ingest-and-observe only: records, metrics, medication lists, appointment history. No booking, no provider message, no medication/regimen execution.
work_items.authority: David is final authority; a health action is an §11 external-real-world action -> Needs-Human-Approval. This task's blocked_paths explicitly forbid any health action here.
work_items.privacy: Health is the most sensitive class: encrypted at rest, strict per-record consent, no sharing to any provider or family member without an explicit consent-backed grant, defined retention and deletion authority, recoverable backup (PERSONAL-001).
work_items.simulation_for_impact: Any proposed health action (appointment, reminder that triggers a real-world side effect, sharing with a provider) runs as a WorldSeed first; the SimulationReport writes constraints.
work_items.approval_for_impact: §11 gate: external real-world action and any sharing of health data are Needs-Human-Approval; nothing leaves the health boundary silently.
work_items.rollback: Snapshot + PITR + revision spine; a granted provider share is revoked by a new revision (valid_to set), never a delete.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord binds simulation, approval, snapshot, and revision before any health workflow closes.
work_items.bounded_next_action: Read-only ingest of health records and metrics into domain_record; produce an action-free health overview. No health action.
work_items.source_grounding: PERSONAL-002 blocked_paths ('health ... action'); §11 (external real-world action -> Needs-Human-Approval); POSTGRES-002 domain_record/blob; PERSONAL-001 privacy family (most-sensitive consent/encryption/deletion).

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (domain_record domain='personal', record_type='legal'/'document'); fs_node + blob for contracts/filings; policy owner: LifeOS Product / Privacy Agent under David's authority
work_items.decision: Read-only legal workflow: ingest contracts, filings, obligations, and deadlines as fs_node/blob/domain_record rows and extract deadlines; take no legal action (no filing, signing, or commitment) without the §11 gate.
work_items.begins_read_only: Ingest-and-observe only: documents (blob content_sha256), contract terms, obligations, deadline extraction. No filing, no signature, no legal commitment.
work_items.authority: David is final authority; Constitution §6 'no legal bypass'. Any legal action is an §11 legal action -> Needs-Human-Approval.
work_items.privacy: Legal documents are privileged and owner-scoped: encrypted, consent-gated, retained per obligation, never shared without an explicit consent-backed grant (PERSONAL-001).
work_items.simulation_for_impact: Any proposed legal action (filing, signing, contract action, deadline commitment) runs as a WorldSeed first; the SimulationReport surfaces failure nodes (missed-deadline, invalid-filing) as constraints.
work_items.approval_for_impact: §11 gate: legal actions are automatic Needs-Human-Approval; approval requires bounded + reversible + evidence-sufficient.
work_items.rollback: Snapshot + PITR + revision spine; the source document blob is immutable by SHA-256, so read-only ingest is non-destructive.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord binds simulation, approval, snapshot, and revision before any legal workflow closes.
work_items.bounded_next_action: Read-only ingest of legal documents into fs_node/blob and extraction of upcoming deadlines. No legal action.
work_items.source_grounding: Master Plan MVP1 domain = legal; Constitution §6 (no legal bypass); §11 (legal -> Needs-Human-Approval); PERSONAL-002 blocked_paths ('legal action'); POSTGRES-002 fs_node/blob/domain_record.

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (fs_node family) + blob (PgStore, redb source-blob store transitional); policy owner: LifeOS Data Agent under David's authority
work_items.decision: Read-only files workflow: catalog user-space files as fs_node rows keyed by (root_id, relative_path) with blob content_sha256; no write, move, rename, or delete without a snapshot and, for destructive ops, the §11 gate.
work_items.begins_read_only: Index-and-catalog only: node metadata (mode, mtime, size, module_path, ordinal) and content identity (blob_ref = SHA-256). No write, move, rename, overwrite, or delete.
work_items.authority: Owner_principal_id holds authority; write/move/delete require a grant. Constitution §6 'no production mutation without snapshot'.
work_items.privacy: Files are encrypted at rest, local-first; per-file/per-tree grants; retention and deletion authority held by the owner (PERSONAL-001).
work_items.simulation_for_impact: Any proposed mutation (move, dedup, cleanup, delete) runs as a WorldSeed producing projected tree diffs; the SimulationReport writes constraints.
work_items.approval_for_impact: §11 gate: destructive/irreversible file operations (delete, overwrite) are Needs-Human-Approval; no mutation without snapshot.
work_items.rollback: revision spine + BlobStore content immutability (SHA-256) reconstruct the exact prior tree and bytes via envctl exact-byte materialization; PITR restores the catalog.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord binds simulation, approval, snapshot, and the resulting fs_node revision before any file mutation closes.
work_items.bounded_next_action: Read-only catalog of user-space files into fs_node with content_sha256; no filesystem mutation.
work_items.source_grounding: POSTGRES-002 fs_node + blob families; STORE-001 verified BlobStore SHA-256 identity; Constitution §6 (no mutation without snapshot); PERSONAL-001 privacy family.

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (domain_record domain='personal', record_type='event'); policy owner: LifeOS Product / Privacy Agent under David's authority
work_items.decision: Read-only calendar workflow: ingest events as domain_record rows; no event create, modify, cancel, or invite-send (a send crosses into communication) without the §11 gate.
work_items.begins_read_only: Ingest-and-observe only: events, times, attendees, recurrence. No create, modify, cancel, or invite send.
work_items.authority: David is final authority; creating events with external side effects or sending invites is an §11 external-real-world/communication-send action -> Needs-Human-Approval.
work_items.privacy: Calendar data is owner-scoped, encrypted, consent-gated; attendee identities are contacts PII; no sharing without an explicit grant (PERSONAL-001).
work_items.simulation_for_impact: Any proposed schedule change (create/move/cancel/invite) runs as a WorldSeed; the SimulationReport surfaces conflicts and downstream impact as constraints.
work_items.approval_for_impact: §11 gate: event creation that sends invites is a communication-send action (blocked here) and any external side effect is Needs-Human-Approval.
work_items.rollback: Snapshot + PITR + revision spine restore prior calendar state; a cancelled/moved event is a new revision, the original preserved.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord binds simulation, approval, snapshot, and revision before any calendar mutation closes.
work_items.bounded_next_action: Read-only ingest of calendar events into domain_record; produce a conflict view. No event mutation, no invite send.
work_items.source_grounding: POSTGRES-002 domain_record (record_type=event) + communication; PERSONAL-002 blocked_paths ('communication send'); §11 approval gate; PERSONAL-001 privacy family.

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (domain_record domain='personal', record_type='contact'; principal for person identities); policy owner: LifeOS Product / Privacy Agent under David's authority
work_items.decision: Read-only contacts workflow: ingest contacts as domain_record rows (with principal identities for people); no create, edit, merge, or outbound use (messaging a contact is a communication send) without the §11 gate.
work_items.begins_read_only: Ingest-and-observe only: names, handles, relationships, and dedup candidates. No create, edit, merge, or message.
work_items.authority: Owner_principal_id holds authority; outbound use (messaging a contact) is a communication-send action -> §11 Needs-Human-Approval (blocked here).
work_items.privacy: Contacts are PII: owner-scoped, encrypted, consent-gated, retention and deletion authority held by the owner; no sharing without an explicit grant (PERSONAL-001).
work_items.simulation_for_impact: Any proposed change (merge, dedup, edit, outreach) runs as a WorldSeed; the SimulationReport writes constraints (e.g. merge-collision failure nodes).
work_items.approval_for_impact: §11 gate: merges/edits are reversible-but-bounded (approval on the simulated diff); any outbound message is a communication-send Needs-Human-Approval action.
work_items.rollback: Snapshot + PITR + revision spine; a merge is reversible as a new revision, prior contact rows preserved.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord binds simulation, approval, snapshot, and revision before any contacts mutation closes.
work_items.bounded_next_action: Read-only ingest of contacts into domain_record with dedup candidates surfaced. No mutation, no outreach.
work_items.source_grounding: POSTGRES-002 domain_record (record_type=contact) + principal; PERSONAL-002 blocked_paths ('communication send'); §11 approval gate; PERSONAL-001 privacy family (PII).

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (communication family); redb buffers transient traces, envctl flushes only settled messages; policy owner: LifeOS Product / Privacy Agent under David's authority
work_items.decision: Read-only communications workflow: ingest message threads as communication rows (thread_id, seq_no, body_sha256); no send or reply — drafts only — until the §11 gate is passed.
work_items.begins_read_only: Ingest-and-observe only: threads, messages (body_sha256), attachments (blob refs). No send, no reply; a proposed reply is a draft, never dispatched.
work_items.authority: David is final authority; any send is an §11 external-real-world action -> Needs-Human-Approval and is explicitly blocked in this task ('communication send').
work_items.privacy: Message content is owner-scoped, encrypted, consent-gated, retained per policy; redactions are new revisions with originals preserved; no thread shared without an explicit grant (PERSONAL-001).
work_items.simulation_for_impact: Any proposed send/reply runs as a WorldSeed producing the draft and projected recipients; the SimulationReport writes constraints (e.g. wrong-recipient failure node).
work_items.approval_for_impact: §11 gate: sending any message is automatic Needs-Human-Approval; approval requires bounded + reversible-in-intent + evidence-sufficient before dispatch.
work_items.rollback: Because nothing is sent read-only, rollback covers drafts (discard) and durable message rows (revision spine + PITR). A dispatched message cannot be unsent, which is exactly why the §11 gate precedes it.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord binds the draft, simulation, approval decision, and revision before any send workflow is considered complete.
work_items.bounded_next_action: Read-only ingest of communication threads into the communication family; surface unread/important. No send, no reply.
work_items.source_grounding: POSTGRES-002 communication family (only settled traces flushed from redb, STORE-001-B boundary); PERSONAL-002 blocked_paths ('communication send'); §11 external-action gate; PERSONAL-001 privacy family.

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (domain_record record_type='note'/'document'; graph_edge for links; semantic_embedding for hybrid search); policy owner: LifeOS Product / Privacy Agent under David's authority
work_items.decision: Read-only knowledge workflow: ingest notes, documents, and references as domain_record rows, build the RuVector semantic index and graph_edge links (read); no bulk edit, reorganization, or deletion without a snapshot and the §11 gate.
work_items.begins_read_only: Ingest-and-index only: notes/documents (body_sha256), semantic_embedding, and belongs-to/references graph edges. No edit, reorganization, or delete.
work_items.authority: Owner_principal_id holds authority; bulk edits/deletion require a grant and 'no production mutation without snapshot' (Constitution §6).
work_items.privacy: Knowledge is owner-scoped, encrypted, consent-gated, retained and deletable by the owner; no sharing without an explicit grant (PERSONAL-001).
work_items.simulation_for_impact: Any proposed knowledge write (summarize, reorganize, merge, delete) runs as a WorldSeed producing projected diffs over a COW branch; the SimulationReport writes constraints and a failed reorganization becomes a failure capsule.
work_items.approval_for_impact: §11 gate: bulk edits and deletions require approval on the simulated COW-branch diff; irreversible deletion is Needs-Human-Approval.
work_items.rollback: COW branch_id isolation + revision spine + PITR: an unmerged staging branch is discarded, a merged change is reversible to its prior revision.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord binds the SimulationReport, the COW merge selection, approval, snapshot, and revision before any knowledge mutation closes.
work_items.bounded_next_action: Read-only ingest of the personal knowledge base into domain_record with a semantic index and link graph built. No edit, no deletion.
work_items.source_grounding: POSTGRES-002 domain_record + graph_edge + semantic_embedding (No-Sidecar RuVector); blueprint COW branching + semantic search; PERSONAL-001 privacy family.

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (domain_record record_type='task'; planning_record for control-plane tasks); policy owner: LifeOS Product / Privacy Agent under David's authority
work_items.decision: Read-only tasks workflow: ingest personal tasks/todos as domain_record rows and surface priorities; no create, complete, or delegation that triggers a real-world action without the §11 gate, and no unlimited agent authority.
work_items.begins_read_only: Ingest-and-observe only: tasks, due dates, priorities, dependencies. No create, complete, or delegate-with-side-effects.
work_items.authority: Owner holds authority; delegation to an agent is bounded — no agent receives unlimited authority (runtime rule); a task that triggers a real-world action escalates through the §11 gate.
work_items.privacy: Task content is owner-scoped, encrypted, consent-gated, retained and deletable by the owner (PERSONAL-001).
work_items.simulation_for_impact: Any proposed task action that has an external effect (a task whose completion spends, sends, or acts) runs as a WorldSeed; the SimulationReport writes constraints, mirroring the compiler mental model (intent -> constraints -> simulated futures -> task graph).
work_items.approval_for_impact: §11 gate: a task that would spend, send, or take a real-world action inherits that domain's Needs-Human-Approval requirement; pure planning edits are bounded + reversible.
work_items.rollback: Snapshot + PITR + revision spine; a completed/reopened task is a new revision, prior state preserved.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord binds simulation, approval, snapshot, and revision before any task action with external effect closes.
work_items.bounded_next_action: Read-only ingest of personal tasks into domain_record with a prioritized, action-free view. No task execution.
work_items.source_grounding: POSTGRES-002 domain_record (record_type=task) + planning_record; Master Plan runtime rule (no unlimited authority) + compiler mental model; PERSONAL-001 privacy family.

work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (domain_record across shared personal records; grant family for cross-principal sharing; principal for family members); policy owner: LifeOS Product / Privacy Agent under David's authority
work_items.decision: Read-only family-coordination workflow: read shared calendar/tasks/expenses already inside existing consent as domain_record rows; no new share/grant to a family member and no send until explicit consent and the §11 gate are passed.
work_items.begins_read_only: Ingest-and-observe only: shared items already covered by an existing consent-backed grant. No new grant, no share creation, no send.
work_items.authority: David is final authority; cross-principal sharing requires an explicit consent-backed grant (PERSONAL-001 invariant: no personal data leaves its boundary silently). Sharing is an §11 action.
work_items.privacy: Per-family-member consent and per-member grants; each member's data stays owner-scoped and encrypted; deletion authority and boundary preserved; a share is scoped and revocable (PERSONAL-001).
work_items.simulation_for_impact: Any proposed share/grant or coordination message runs as a WorldSeed showing exactly which records would cross to which member; the SimulationReport writes constraints (over-share failure node).
work_items.approval_for_impact: §11 gate: creating a share/grant to another family member requires that member's consent and David's approval; any coordination message is a communication-send action (blocked here).
work_items.rollback: Grant revocation is a new revision (valid_to set), never a delete; PITR + revision spine restore the pre-share access-control state.
work_items.proof: ProofRecord binds the SimulationReport, the consent record, the approval decision, the snapshot, and the grant revision before any share workflow closes.
work_items.bounded_next_action: Read-only ingest of shared family-coordination items already within existing consent into domain_record. No new share, no grant, no send.
work_items.source_grounding: POSTGRES-002 domain_record + grant + principal + communication; PERSONAL-001 invariant (no data leaves boundary silently; sharing authority); §11 approval gate; runtime rule (final human authority).

execution_order_rationale: Read-only, lowest-blast-radius domains first (files, knowledge, tasks, contacts, calendar), then the communication surface, then the impact-bearing MVP1 domains (finance, legal) and the most-sensitive health, closing with family coordination which composes grants across the prior domains.
completion_gate: generated/task_families/PERSONAL-002.json defines all ten personal domains, each of which (1) begins read-only, (2) names explicit authority, (3) names explicit privacy, (4) defines DevWorld simulation for impact, (5) defines a §11 Needs-Human-Approval step for impact, (6) includes rollback, (7) includes proof, and (8) includes a single bounded next action — and proof_records/PERSONAL-002.proof.json records that no personal service or data was accessed and no product execution was performed.
rollback_plan: Restore the archived personal-domain plan; no personal service or data is changed.
downstream: PERSONAL-003 (Personal operating environment acceptance plan) consumes these ten read-only workflow families as the acceptance surface.
POSTGRES-004 (collectors + lossless reconstruction test) defines the archive-first collectors these read-only ingests run on.
POSTGRES-005 (embedding + semantic edit) defines the semantic_embedding pipeline the knowledge domain's read index uses.
